def: An under bed or mattress of straw. [Written also palliasse.]
Not content; discontented; dissatisfied. Jer. Taylor.Passion seemed to be much discontent, but Patience was very quiet.Bunyan.
Induration of the tissues. See Sclerma, Scleroderma, andSclerosis.
Of or pertaining to Phoenica.-- n.
Like coral, or partaking of its qualities.
Imbricated, with the overlapping ends directed downward.

To dissolve the union or connection of; to disunite; to sever;to separate; to disperse.The commonwealth itself would . . . be disconnected into the dust andpowder of individuality. Burke.This restriction disconnects bank paper and the precious metals.Walsh.
The back or upper surface, as of a bird.
